Description: Builds actionable source sequences and parallel volume/sample-ID arrays from the normalized sample table. Splits samples into two processing groups based on required sample volume: a 10 L tip group and a 50 L tip group, while also generating destination indexing metadata for downstream diluent additions and final transfer. Key actions: Loops through each table row: Reads SAMPLEID, LABWAREID, INDEX, NORM_SAMPLEVOL, NORM_DILUENTVOL. Adds to ALL sequence. Routes into 10 L or 50 L sequence based on sample volume threshold. Stores sample volumes and sample IDs in parallel arrays. Uses STCC::SetElementBarcodeForSequencePos() to push sample IDs into sample-tracking. Computes destination indexing so later steps can target the correct regions of the dilution plate. Results: Establishes the ordering relationship between sequences, arrays, and destination plate positions.
Variable Description: [out] Variable with the intager of the sequence start position for samples needing under 50uL transfers
[out] Array of Sample Volume needed for 10uL transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Variable with the intager of the number of positions for samples needing under 50uL transfers
[out] Array of Diluent Volume needed for 10uL sample transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Variable with the intager of the sequence start position for all samples
[out] Sequence of 50uL transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Variable with the intager of the number of positions for all samples
[out] Array of Sample Volume needed for 50uL transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Array of Sample IDs for 50uL transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Array of Diluent Volume needed for 50uL sample transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Variable with the intager of the sequence start position for samples needing under 10uL transfers
[out] Variable with the intager of the number of positions for samples needing under 10uL transfers
[in/out] ArrayTable of samples with their associated information
[out] Sequence of all samples in original plate
[out] Array of Sample IDs for 10uL transfers from samples in original plate
[out] Sequence of 10uL transfers from samples in original plate

Description: Executes the sample transfer step for the large volume group using 50 L tips. Processes the source sequence in channel-aligned batches, performs aspirate/dispense, logs step results into report arrays, and traces aspiration/dispense reports for troubleshooting. Key actions: Sets sequence max/positioning. Aligns channels/tips/source positions via AlignSequences(...). Performs physical pipetting on the instrument. Runs TipPickUp ? Aspirate ? Dispense ? TipEject. Populates reporting arrays via REPORT_ARRAY_STORE::Report_Array_Store(). Captures recoveries and stores results in report arrays. Traces results for audit/debug. Generates trace output: Aspiration report arrays Dispense report arrays SampleID vs drive-adjusted aspirated volume arrays Results: Provides traceable execution and diagnostics for higher-volume transfers. Any sequence/array misalignment will present as incorrect reporting correlation or runtime indexing failures.
Variable Description: [out] Sequence of 50uL transfers from samples in original plate
[in/out] Array of Volumes to pipette for Samples under 50uL
[in/out] Array of Sample IDs from Samples under 50uL

Description: Adds diluent into the destination dilution plate region corresponding to the 50 L sample group. Processes the destination window in aligned batches, performs aspirate/dispense, logs step results into report arrays, and traces aspiration/dispense reports. Key actions: Sets the dilution plate destination start/count window. Aligns channels/tips/destination positions via AlignSequences(...). Performs physical pipetting on the instrument. Runs TipPickUp ? Aspirate ? Dispense ? TipEject. Populates reporting arrays via REPORT_ARRAY_STORE::Report_Array_Store(). Captures recoveries and stores results in report arrays. Traces aspiration/dispense results for audit/debug. Results: Ensures dilution volumes are applied to the correct destination region for the large-volume cohort. Any mismatch between destination windowing and the diluent volume array will surface as incorrect well fills or runtime indexing issues.

Executes the sample transfer step for the small volume group using 10 L low-volume tips. Processes the source sequence in channel-aligned batches, performs aspirate/dispense, logs step results into report arrays, and traces aspiration/dispense reports for troubleshooting. Key actions: Sets sequence max/positioning. Aligns channels/tips/source positions via AlignSequences(...). Performs physical pipetting on the instrument. Runs TipPickUp ? Aspirate ? Dispense ? TipEject. Populates reporting arrays via REPORT_ARRAY_STORE::Report_Array_Store(). Captures recoveries and stores results in report arrays. Traces results for audit/debug. Generates trace output: Aspiration report arrays Dispense report arrays SampleID vs drive-adjusted aspirated volume arrays Results: Any mismatch between expected numeric volumes and the volume array content will surface here as Vector/runtime errors. Produces detailed per-step diagnostic traces for low-volume transfers where performance is most sensitive.
Variable Description: [out] Sequence of 10uL transfers from samples in original plate
[in/out] Array of Volumes to pipette for Samples under 10uL
[in/out] Array of Sample IDs from Samples under 10uL

Description: Adds diluent into the destination dilution plate region corresponding to the 10 L sample group. Processes the destination window in aligned batches using low-volume tips, logs step results into report arrays, and traces aspiration/dispense reports. Key actions: Sets the dilution plate destination start/count window. Aligns channels/tips/destination positions via AlignSequences(...). Performs physical pipetting on the instrument. Runs TipPickUp ? Aspirate ? Dispense ? TipEject. Populates reporting arrays via REPORT_ARRAY_STORE::Report_Array_Store(). Captures recoveries and stores results in report arrays. Traces aspiration/dispense results for audit/debug. Results: Ensures dilution volumes are applied correctly for the low-volume cohort where accuracy and dispense behavior are most sensitive. Any mismatch between destination windowing and array sizing/order will surface as errors or incorrect dilution patterns.

Transfers the final diluted samples from the dilution plate into the Jess plate. Processes the combined destination region, aligns dilution wells to Jess positions, performs aspirate/dispense, logs step results into report arrays, and traces aspiration/dispense reports. Key actions: Sets the dilution plate destination start/count for the full combined region. Aligns dilution plate wells to Jess plate positions via AlignSequences(...). Performs physical pipetting on the instrument. Runs TipPickUp ? Aspirate ? Dispense ? TipEject. Populates reporting arrays via REPORT_ARRAY_STORE::Report_Array_Store(). Captures recoveries and stores results in report arrays. Traces aspiration/dispense results for audit/debug. Results: Produces the final transfer into Jess consumables with full traceability of aspiration/dispense performance. Any ordering mismatch in the combined destination window will present as wrong Jess well mapping and inconsistent reporting correlation.

NOTE: CAN NOT TAKE INPUTS OR RETURNS TO ALLOW FOR THIS FUNCTION TO WORK WITH FORK/JOIN LIBRARY Performs method startup initialization for the instrument and runtime environment. Runs the STAR/VANTAGE Initialize command and a firmware command, then reads simulation mode for conditional behavior. Key actions: Executes instrument initialize step (homes axes / prepares modules). Issues a firmware command to lower the detection for the Quad Core Grippers (QCG). Reads Util::GetSimulationMode() into a local runtime flag for the usage in the method. Result: Establishes instrument-ready state before any labware access, sequence building, or pipetting begins.

Reads the CSV worklist input file and loads each row into an in-memory ArrayTable (t_tbl_SAMPLE_SOURCE). Validates required fields per row and initializes the table with standardized column headers for downstream normalization and tracing. Key actions: Defines input fields (INDEX, LABWARE_ID, SAMPLE_ID, CONC) and opens CSV. For each record: Validates concentration, index, labware id, sample id (fatal if missing). Appends a new row into the ArrayTable. Closes file, removes fields, traces the loaded table via ArrayTable::TraceArrayTablePretty(). Result: Table is created and becomes the single source of truth for sample metadata + later computed volumes.
Variable Description: [in] filepath string where the worklist resdes
[out] Table array of samples with their associated information
[out] Total number of source samples

Description: Calculates normalized transfer volumes for each source sample based on target concentration and desired final volume. Writes computed sample volume and diluent volume back into the ArrayTable columns NORM_SAMPLEVOL and NORM_DILUENTVOL. Key actions: Loops over table rows. Pulls CONCENTRATION and computes: sampleVol = round((TargetConc * FinalVol) / Concentration, 1) dilVol = round(FinalVol - sampleVol, 1) Writes the values back to the table. Traces the updated table for verification. Results: Establishes the numeric values used to decide which tip type to use and how sequences/arrays are built later.
Variable Description: [in/out] ArrayTable of samples with their associated information
[in] Target concentration for all samples to normalize to
[in] Final volume needed for each sample normalization
